cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.13 FATAL_ERROR)

project(TimeMachine CXX)

# The VST3 plugin is a shared module; everything linked into it (incl. the
# static pffft lib) must be position-independent or the Linux link fails with
# "recompile with -fPIC".
set(CMAKE_POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE ON)

include(dependencies.cmake)

add_library(TimeMachine STATIC
  TimeMachine/TimeMachine.cpp
  TimeMachine/TimeMachine.hpp
)

# pffft.h is included by TimeMachine.hpp, so the backend glue generated by
# avnd_make_all needs it too: link/include PUBLIC.
target_link_libraries(TimeMachine PUBLIC PFFFT::PFFFT)

# We ship VST3 only: call the vst3 backend directly rather than avnd_make_all
# (which also builds godot/wasm/standalone/etc. and is not reliably gated by
# the AVND_ENABLE_<BACKEND> options across CMP0077 policy settings).
avnd_make_vst3(
  TARGET TimeMachine
  MAIN_FILE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/TimeMachine/TimeMachine.hpp"
  MAIN_CLASS TimeMachine
  C_NAME avnd_time_machine
)

# On Linux the SDK's pluginterfaces static lib doesn't pull funknown.cpp into
# the module, leaving FUID::generate()/FUID::FUID()/FUnknownPrivate::atomicAdd
# undefined. A shared module links anyway, but hosts (score, Reaper) then fail
# to dlopen it and silently skip the plugin. Compile funknown.cpp directly into
# the module and fail the build on any remaining undefined symbol.
# macOS/Windows already resolve these, so this is Linux-only.
if(UNIX AND NOT APPLE AND VST3_SDK_ROOT)
  target_sources(TimeMachine_vst3
    PRIVATE "${VST3_SDK_ROOT}/pluginterfaces/base/funknown.cpp")
  target_link_options(TimeMachine_vst3 PRIVATE "-Wl,--no-undefined")
endif()
